# NELAIA - AI-First Compiler
# Graph-based language optimized for AI code generation
# https://github.com/NELAIA-AI/nelaia

## CRITICAL - Read First
Location: docs/CRITICAL-PATTERNS.jsonl
Format: JSONL with correct/wrong examples
Purpose: Avoid common mistakes in code generation

Location: docs/COMMON-MISTAKES.md
Format: Markdown with examples
Purpose: Human-readable guide to common errors

IMPORTANT: Servers need loops! Use `.loop >> .loop` for persistent servers.
IMPORTANT: PRT needs TWO arguments: buffer AND length.

## What
NELAIA is a compiler that generates optimal native executables from graph-based source code.
Optimized for AI systems to generate and consume.

## For
AI agents that need to generate compiled programs.
Systems that benefit from token-efficient code generation.
Applications requiring minimal, dependency-free executables.

## Why
Traditional languages have syntax overhead that increases token usage in AI contexts.
NELAIA addresses this with:
- Graph-based paradigm (natural for dependency modeling)
- Token-efficient syntax (minimal tokens, maximum information)
- Tiny output (Hello World = 145 bytes Linux, 1KB Windows)
- No runtime dependencies

## Critical Syntax Rules
1. All nodes start with dot: .name: value
2. PRT requires buffer AND length: PRT .msg .len
3. Servers need loop: .loop >> .loop
4. Declare capabilities: REQUIRES { http }

## Capabilities
- compile_to_windows_pe: Generate Windows x64 executables
- compile_to_linux_elf: Generate Linux x64 executables
- compile_to_macos_macho: Generate macOS x64 (Intel) executables
- compile_to_macos_arm64: Generate macOS ARM64 (Apple Silicon) executables
- http_server: Create HTTP servers
- http_client: Make HTTP requests
- sql_database: Connect to SQL databases (ODBC)
- json_processing: Parse and generate JSON
- file_io: Read and write files
- networking: TCP/UDP sockets
- threading: Multi-threaded execution
- gui_windows: Windows GUI (MessageBox, windows)

## Input Format
.nela files (NELAIA source)
Graph-based syntax with nodes and data flow.

Example:
```
.msg: "Hello World!\n"
.len: 14
.out: PRT .msg .len
```

## Output Format
Native executables:
- Windows: PE format (.exe)
- Linux: ELF format
- macOS: Mach-O format (Intel x64 and ARM64 Apple Silicon)

JSON compilation results available with --json flag.

## Invoke
```
# Basic compilation (auto-detects platform, no external dependencies)
nelaia-c input.nela -o output

# With JSON output (for programmatic use)
nelaia-c input.nela -o output --json

# Force specific format
nelaia-c input.nela --emit-pe    # Windows PE
nelaia-c input.nela --emit-elf   # Linux ELF

# Use LLVM+Clang flow (optional)
nelaia-c input.nela --use-clang
```

## Training Data
Location: docs/NELAIA-TRAINING-DATA.jsonl
Format: JSONL with input/output pairs
Count: 100+ examples

Example entry:
{"input": "Create HTTP server on port 8080", "output": ".caps: REQUIRES { http }\n.server: HTTP.LISTEN 8080\n.req: HTTP.ACCEPT .server"}

## Grammar
Location: docs/NELAIA-REFERENCE-v0.22.md
Format: EBNF

Key syntax:
- Nodes: .id: VALUE or .id: OPERATION .args
- Flow: .a > .b (data flow)
- Capabilities: REQUIRES { cap1, cap2 }

## Semantic Rules
Location: docs/NELAIA-SEMANTICS-v0.22.md
Contains: Type signatures, constraints, error codes

## Examples
Location: docs/NELAIA-EXAMPLES-v0.22.md
Format: Structured examples with dependency graphs

## Error Codes
Structured error format for programmatic handling.
See: docs/NELAIA-SEMANTICS-v0.22.md#error-codes

Current: 0.23
